sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Sevilla have shown strong form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, including a convincing 4-1 victory over Barcelona. Mallorca, on the other hand, are struggling, with only 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their recent games, sitting at the bottom of the league table with just 5 points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Sevilla and Mallorca have been tight, resulting in a 1-1 draw and a 0-0 stalemate. Sevilla remain unbeaten in their last 3 head-to-head games, but Mallorca have shown resilience in avoiding heavy defeats.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Sevilla rank high in goal-scoring (3rd in the league with 15 goals) and tackling (1st), though they underperform in expected goals (20th). Mallorca, positioned 20th, struggle offensively with just 7 goals and have conceded 13, indicating defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Sevilla's ability to score early (first to score in 5 of their last 6 games) and their high foul count (2nd in the league) could disrupt Mallorca’s rhythm. Mallorca’s tendency to concede first (4 of last 5 games) and poor away form suggest they’ll struggle at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sevilla’s superior form, league position (7th), and home advantage, they are clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore’s 66% home win prediction. Mallorca’s defensive frailties and lack of attacking threat make an upset unlikely, though they might aim for a low-scoring draw as seen in recent head-to-heads.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Sevilla leads Mallorca by a narrow margin with a 1-0 scoreline at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán. The first half saw Sevilla take an early advantage through a well-taken goal by Ruben Vargas in the 16th minute. Both teams showed moments of promise, but Sevilla's ability to capitalize on their chance has given them the edge. The home side dominated possession and created more structured attacks, while Mallorca struggled to convert their opportunities despite pushing forward at times.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla displayed a strong presence in the first half, controlling the ball and creating a notable big chance that resulted in their goal. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area reflect a proactive approach, complemented by a solid performance in aerial and ground duels. Mallorca, on the other hand, managed to penetrate the final third quite often and even created a big chance, but a missed opportunity has left them trailing. Their slightly lower possession and duel-winning percentages suggest they need to be more assertive to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Sevilla dictate the attacking tempo early on, with sustained pressure in the opening minutes that set the tone for the game. Their momentum peaked around the time of the goal, with a flurry of attacking intent that kept Mallorca on the back foot. While Mallorca responded with a few spells of their own in the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent, they couldn’t maintain consistency. Towards the end of the half, Sevilla regained control with another strong surge, though Mallorca showed late signs of life with increased attacking presence in the closing stages.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Sevilla, Ruben Vargas has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat in attack. José Ángel Carmona also made a significant impact with an assist and a high rating, showing creativity down the flank. On the Mallorca side, Jan Virgili has been their most dangerous player, getting into promising positions and testing the Sevilla defense. His ability to capitalize on chances in the second half could be crucial for a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla's approach in the first half relied on maintaining possession and building play through the final third, which paid off with their goal. Their defense has held firm, with players like Gabriel Suazo contributing both offensively and defensively. Mallorca, however, needs to improve their finishing and perhaps commit more numbers forward to break through Sevilla’s backline. Adjusting their setup to exploit spaces on the counter could be key, as they’ve shown they can enter dangerous areas but lack the final product.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead, Sevilla will likely aim to protect their lead while seeking to extend it through controlled possession and quick transitions. Mallorca, trailing by one, must adopt a more aggressive stance to level the score, potentially leaving gaps at the back. Given the attacking intent both teams displayed at various points, the second half could see more goals as Mallorca pushes for an equalizer and Sevilla looks to capitalize on any defensive lapses.
